message:
test(unistd): track aio_suspend pointer-type tightening in negative tests

aio_suspend's declared ABI was tightened in a prior commit so that it
now takes `*const *const libc::aiocb` directly rather than the
`*const *const c_void` shape it used during early scaffolding. The
three negative timespec tests in
`crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/unistd_abi_test.rs`
(`aio_suspend_rejects_negative_tv_sec_without_panic`,
`aio_suspend_rejects_negative_tv_nsec`, and
`aio_suspend_rejects_oversize_tv_nsec`) still cast the list base with
`list.as_ptr() as *const *const c_void`, which is now a redundant
cast through a weaker type.

This commit drops the cast so the call site passes `list.as_ptr()`
directly to aio_suspend; rustc now type-checks the pointer element
type rather than letting a c_void cast hide a future mismatch. No
behavioural change in the test — all three cases still assert that
EINVAL (rc == -1) is returned without panicking, matching the
bd-4rdz8 aio timespec validation fix.

Also includes the companion `cargo fmt` normalization of this file's
top-level `use frankenlibc_abi::unistd_abi::{ ... }` list: rustfmt
resorts the alphabetical groups (so `access, aio_suspend,
arc4random_buf, ...` sit together at the top) and re-wraps the
`fts_*` re-export lines and the trailing `wordexp as abi_wordexp,
wordfree as abi_wordfree, write,` cluster. Pure ordering; no symbol
added or removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
